digest.greeting: Tuesday, June 16, Jean. As of 9:56 AM EDT, the day has shifted from a normal meeting-heavy Tuesday into a tighter operating day: the Staff Augmentation escalation now needs a clear IT-alignment path, the Venn ticket collection is active in Teams, and the 4:00 PM SUPP/post-submission scoping call now has live overlap/priority context from email. Outlook shows 7 meetings plus a protected block, with 5 open items carried forward from this morning and no active reminders left in state. Teams broad-thread discovery still returned a Microsoft Graph 403, so the Teams readout uses targeted unread chats only.

items.label: Collect Venn ticket numbers for one escalation path
items.detail: Still open from this morning and now active in Teams. Jean told Mar she would ask the group for ticket numbers so the Venn issues can be escalated together. Mar also said Venn warned they may close her ticket because they thought there had been no recent update, even though she had been working with another support contact. The useful next step is to collect each TM's ticket number, current blocker, and whether Venn is threatening closure.

items.label: Confirm the leadership project-wins summary
items.detail: Carried from this morning. Nadia asked Yuri and Javier for short project-win statements for Sara and Danny's June 17 leadership presentation, including Staff Augmentation. Because Staff Aug now has a stronger escalation thread, confirm whether the win/risk language still fits or needs to be updated before tomorrow.

items.label: Close the Frank report-requirements task
items.detail: Carried from this morning. Planner showed 'Check w/ Frank the report requirements' due today. Use the Yuri 1:1 or a quick follow-up to confirm what report fields or folder requirements are still unclear and who owns the next step.

items.label: Turn PM Workshop notes into named next steps
items.detail: Carried from this morning. The PM Workshop is about stakeholder control and is followed immediately by Yuri and Jhara 1:1s. Capture the practical takeaway, then use the 1:1s to name owners for any portfolio roadblocks instead of leaving them as broad discussion points.

items.label: Follow up with David on the COE playbook
items.detail: Carried from the scheduled reminder shown earlier today. David accepted the 11:30 AM 1:1 after an automatic reply said he was returning today, so use that meeting to confirm the playbook owner, expected output, and timing.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times shown in Jean's working timezone. The strongest remaining prep space is the 12:00-1:30 PM protected block; after 2:00 PM the calendar runs straight through scoping work.

items.label: 1:1 Conor B. & Jean G.
items.detail: This was in progress around the time of the updated run. The agenda is department needs, Jean's support, and project-management principles for better outcomes.

items.label: 1:1 David & Jean
items.detail: Newly visible in today's calendar window. Good moment to close the COE playbook reminder because David accepted after his automatic reply said he was returning today.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Best remaining protected time. Use it for Staff Aug escalation notes, Venn ticket-number collection, and scoping-call questions before the afternoon compresses.

items.label: PM Workshop Pt.2
items.detail: Topic is stakeholder control. Treat it as setup for the Yuri and Jhara 1:1s, not as a standalone workshop.

items.label: 1:1 Yuri & Jean
items.detail: Use this for Staff Aug risk language, Frank/report requirements, and any owner questions before tomorrow's Staff Aug touchpoint.

items.label: 1:1 Jhara & Jean
items.detail: Agenda includes portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps. Bring the case-change overlap and any post-workshop blockers that need a named owner.

items.label: Scoping Call: RNWLS - Missing EAD Inquiry & SUPP - Post-submission Case Change
items.detail: Jessica is the attendee, and her email today argues the post-submission case-change issue is urgent even if it overlaps an older broader initiative. Prep the decision question before the call: consolidate, reprioritize, or keep this as a narrow urgent path.

items.label: Clarify the Staff Augmentation IT ask
items.detail: Nadia sent a new Staff Augmentation thread for Josh, Deepak, Evan, and Jean. The message says the tracker is still manual, court-case volume is rising, and the long-term solution is blocked because IT alignment and ownership are not assigned. Before the Yuri 1:1 or tomorrow's Staff Aug touchpoint, decide what Jean needs to confirm: the exact IT owner, the Rippling/M365 integration scope, and whether the escalation language is ready for leadership.

items.label: Decide how to route the case-change overlap
items.detail: Jessica replied that the SUPP post-submission case-change need should stay prioritized because the existing process does not work for the urgent Phase 2 gap. Jorge's earlier email suggested closing it as overlap with Laura's broader case-change initiative while taking the urgency back to Jill. The 4:00 PM scoping call needs a practical path: consolidate the work, ask Jill to reprioritize the broader project, or keep a narrow urgent SUPP fix moving.

items.label: Clear the overdue Rippling training notice
items.detail: Rippling sent a new unread notice showing one overdue task: Default Harassment Prevention Training for EOR EEs in the Dominican Republic, due December 9, 2025. This looks administrative rather than strategic, but it is overdue and should be cleared or delegated so it stops resurfacing.

items.label: Clarify what is waiting on Joe and Azul
items.detail: In the Queixinhas group chat, Jean said the group is waiting on 'lo de Joe y de Azul,' and Jhara asked what that means. If this is tied to an active project or today's roadblock conversations, clarify the specific dependency so it does not stay ambiguous before the afternoon 1:1s.

items.label: TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint
items.detail: Now more important because of today's Staff Aug escalation thread. Bring the IT-owner question, integration scope, and leadership-ready risk language.

items.label: Jean/Sara 1:1
items.detail: Good place to confirm the leadership-presentation summary and whether Staff Aug should be framed as a win, risk, or escalation.

items.label: EPMO Team Meeting
items.detail: Operational update forum for project spotlights, completed initiatives, new initiatives, and EPMO guidance. Useful if the Venn escalation, case-change routing, or Staff Aug update is ready for a wider audience.

items.label: Focus time: weekly meeting preparation
items.detail: End-of-day prep block for Thursday and Friday meetings.
